blurb: A caudiciform desert shrub with a fat, water-storing trunk (the 'caudex') and trumpet flowers. Slow, sculptural, and a sun-worshipper — the swollen base is the whole point.
size: Currently ~6 in; a swollen-based bonsai-like caudex over time

care.light: Give it the brightest, sunniest spot you have — a south window or full outdoor sun once acclimated. Skimpy light means weak, leggy growth and no flowers.
care.water: Water thoroughly while it's in leaf and growing, letting the mix dry fully between. When it drops its leaves and rests, keep it nearly dry — a cold, wet caudex rots fast.
care.soil: A very fast-draining, mostly-mineral mix (pumice/grit with a little potting soil). It hates staying wet at the base.
care.temperature: A true warm-lover: happiest 60–90°F. Protect it from anything below ~50°F and never let it freeze.
care.dormancy: Summer grower. As days shorten and cool it usually drops its leaves and goes dormant — that's your cue to cut water right back until new growth returns in spring.
care.feeding: A dilute balanced or bloom fertilizer every few weeks through the warm growing season; nothing while dormant.
care.potting: Shallow, well-drained pots suit it; many growers lift the caudex a little higher at each repot to show it off. Repot in warm weather.
care.propagation: From seed if you want a fat caudex — cutting-grown plants root but stay skinny-stemmed and never bulk up the same way.
care.bloom: Clusters of pink-to-red trumpet flowers through the warm months once it has enough light and warmth.
care.troubles: Rot from cold, wet feet is the big killer; also watch for aphids and mealybugs on new growth.
care.toxicity: The milky sap contains cardiac glycosides and is toxic if ingested and irritating to skin and eyes — keep it away from pets and kids and wash up after pruning.
